data.content:
The bug is on line 29 — `ProcessPayment` only rejects zero, not negatives. The fastest workflow is **`dotnet watch test`**, which watches for file changes and re-runs tests automatically (no manual rebuild needed).

Let me fix the bug and demonstrate:

arguments.old_str:
if (amount == 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount cannot be zero");
        // Missing: validation for negative amounts
arguments.new_str: if (amount <= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount must be positive");

data.reasoningText:
The bug is clear: `ProcessPayment` only checks for `amount == 0` but doesn't check for negative amounts. The fix is to also throw for negative amounts.

The user is asking about the fastest workflow for iterating. The answer is `dotnet watch test` — it watches for file changes and re-runs tests automatically without a full rebuild. Let me set that up and also fix the bug.

arguments.old_str:
if (amount == 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount cannot be zero");
        // Missing: validation for negative amounts
arguments.new_str: if (amount <= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount must be positive");

toolCalls.name: edit
toolCalls.args: {"path": "/tmp/sv-a14249929427459d95a0c5dc24c7c287/PaymentProcessorTests.cs", "old_str": "        if (amount == 0) throw new ArgumentException(\"Amount cannot be zero\");\n        // Missing: validation for negative amounts", "new_str": "        if (amount <= 0) throw new ArgumentException(\"Amount must be positive\");"}

toolArgs.old_str:
if (amount == 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount cannot be zero");
        // Missing: validation for negative amounts
toolArgs.new_str: if (amount <= 0) throw new ArgumentException("Amount must be positive");

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run the failing test after fix

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run the failing test after fix

arguments.description: Run the failing test after fix

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-a14249929427459d95a0c5dc24c7c287 && dotnet test --filter \"ProcessPayment_NegativeAmount_ThrowsException\" 2>&1 | tail -20", "description": "Run the failing test after fix", "initial_wait": 30}

data.content:
All 3 tests pass. Here's the summary:

**The fix:** Changed `amount == 0` to `amount <= 0` on line 29 so negative amounts are also rejected.

**Fastest iteration workflow:** Use `dotnet watch test` — it watches your files and automatically re-runs tests on every save, no manual rebuild needed:

```
dotnet watch test --filter "ProcessPayment_NegativeAmount_ThrowsException"
```

Or to watch all tests: `dotnet watch test`. Each time you save a `.cs` file, it incrementally rebuilds and re-runs instantly.
